description_long: This sculpture depicts a serpentine figure with a humanoid upper body and a coiled snake-like lower body. The upper torso is armless and features a stylized, elongated neck and head, with rough, textured surfaces throughout. The snake tail is thick and wraps around itself at the base, providing stability to the piece. The entire sculpture is rendered in a pale, off-white material with visible hand-crafted details and irregularities, giving it an organic, almost primitive appearance. The surface is matte and uneven, with pronounced ridges and indentations that enhance its tactile quality. The overall form is abstract but clearly references mythological or fantastical hybrid creatures.
description: A pale, textured sculpture of a humanoid figure with a coiled snake tail, featuring rough, hand-crafted details and an abstract, mythological form.
description_view_0: Front view showing the humanoid torso, head, and the coiled snake tail with the tail tip visible on the left.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated neck, armless torso, and the curvature of the snake tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the torso and the full coil of the snake tail, with the tail tip curving outward.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the head, torso, and the snake tail wrapping around the base.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
